title: Gomphia oblongifolia
intro: Gomphia oblongifolia is a plant species belonging to the family Ochnaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Gomphia oblongifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Gomphia oblongifolia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of various herbal remedies, including teas, tonics, and capsules.
taxonomy: Gomphia oblongifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Olacaceae. It belongs to the genus Gomphia, which consists of tropical plants found in Central and West Africa. The plant's classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Its classification also includes the class Magnoliopsida and the order Santalales. This classification places Gomphia oblongifolia among other tropical and subtropical plant species.
